events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to diagnose a fault: "splice outside box". The work is in-service, electrical terminations & splices. Reference: NEC 110.14. The condition is a splice outside a box. That is a code violation. NEC 110.14 likely requires splices to be in boxes or enclosures. So the decision is fail. Risk: high or critical? A splice outside a box is exposed, could cause shock or fire. In-service, so it's an immediate hazard. Risk: critical. Work stage: in-service. Component conditions: non-compliant, maybe degraded or failed? The splice itself might be degraded if exposed, but the condition is "splice outside box", so the component (the splice) is non-compliant. Could also be "installed-defective" if it was never in a box. I'll list "non-compliant" and maybe "degraded" if it's been exposed. But the scenario says "observed field condition: splice outside box". So it's a non-compliant installation. Percent complete: 100? It's in-service, so presumably the work is complete, but the splice is defective. I'll set percent_complete to 100. Value: 0. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Splice located outside of an approved enclosure, violating NEC 110.14." Actions: "Immediately de-energize circuit, install proper junction box, and re-terminate splice inside box." Rationale: "NEC 110.14 requires splices to be made in enclosures. An exposed splice poses shock and fire hazards." References: ["NEC 110.14"].

I'll output JSON.
